evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis log-loss + cosine LR clearly shown; color-coded axes per brief hint; monotonic loss decrease and cosine decay both visible.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=none set; pdffonts reports Type 3 (bitmap glyphs); SVG has 0 <text> elements — all text converted to paths; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for loss, red (#d62728) for LR; lines also distinguished by solid vs dashed style; palette is colorblind-safe and directionally neutral for this use case.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,4) with tight_layout; axes fill the panel well; no overlapping labels; y-range auto-fitted to data with ax2 bottom=0 for LR.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and color coding; legend placed upper-right but overlaps the loss curve's early high values; no data.json included in deliverables.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 fonts (glyph outlines, not selectable text); script omits matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 and rcParams['svg.fonttype']='none'.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (17 KB), figure.png (71 KB), caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; figure.svg also produced; data.json input file absent from directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numerical values (loss 2.19→0.21, LR 10^-3→0), names both axes and their scales, and explains the color-coding convention.

overall_strengths: Scientific content is complete and well-executed: dual-axis design, log scale, color-coupled axis labels, and dashed LR line all follow the brief's hints.
Caption is quantitatively specific with start/end values for both series and explicitly describes the visual encoding.
overall_weaknesses: Text is not editable in either PDF or SVG output because pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are not set, producing Type 3 bitmap glyphs instead of selectable TrueType text.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used with no rcParams override; legend placement at upper-right partially overlaps the high-loss region of the curve.
